spec_match.rationale: The studio constructs correct, valid CSS comma-separated @container syntax matching the W3C CSSContain 3 drafts.

interactive.rationale: Excellent interface. Users can add named container clauses, toggle operator directions, set trigger values, choose the triggered style property, copy code blocks, and drag sliders.

edge_cases.rationale: The live preview is driven by a JavaScript simulation (queries.some(...)) that updates the card's element inline style. It never populates the <style id="dynamic-style"> tag, completely bypassing native CSS parsing and layout checks.

design_system.rationale: Clean, professional panel layout with compact controls, high contrast borders, and copy-to-clipboard functionality.

openQuestions.title: Use native CSS injection for live preview when supported
openQuestions.detail: The studio could inject the generated CSS block into <style id="dynamic-style"> and let the browser evaluate the container query natively. The JS-driven manual evaluation should only act as a fallback for browsers without native comma-separated query support.

summary: A highly interactive visual rule builder generating correct CSS blocks, though its preview mechanism relies entirely on a JS simulator rather than native CSS sheet rule insertion.
